description: Tests whether the eval skill (1) matches spec section 10.3 format and (2) the pre-prompt hook correctly identifies token-wasting prompts

evals.name: eval-report-from-realistic-log
evals.prompt: Run /lqc-tokens:eval and show me my token savings report. I've been using the plugin for about 2 weeks.
evals.context: A ~/.claude/lqc-tokens-baseline.json and ~/.claude/lqc-tokens-log.jsonl will be pre-created in the test environment with realistic data: 14 sessions, ~30% token reduction, 2 docker adoptions (1 FalkorDB, 1 Postgres), 1 hygiene session reset, 72% hook acceptance.
evals.expected_output: Report in spec 10.3 format with all 5 metric lines (Token savings, Cache hit rate, Hook acceptance, DB advisor, Session hygiene) plus one adaptive recommendation.

assertions.text: Report contains all 5 required metric lines from spec 10.3: Token savings, Cache hit rate, Hook acceptance, DB advisor, Session hygiene
Token savings line shows a negative percentage (improvement) given the test data where post-install avg is 30% lower
DB advisor line shows breakdown by DB type (FalkorDB and Postgres) matching the test log data
Report uses the exact separator line of dashes from spec 10.3 (not a different format)
One and only one adaptive recommendation is shown at the end

evals.name: pre-prompt-hook-large-data-paste
evals.prompt:
I have this sales data for Q4 and need you to analyze it:

date,region,product,units,revenue,cost
2024-10-01,EMEA,Widget-A,142,28400,18460
2024-10-01,EMEA,Widget-B,87,26100,14790
2024-10-01,APAC,Widget-A,203,40600,26390
2024-10-02,EMEA,Widget-A,118,23600,15340
2024-10-02,APAC,Widget-B,165,49500,28050
2024-10-03,AMER,Widget-A,310,62000,40300
2024-10-03,AMER,Widget-B,198,59400,33660
2024-10-04,EMEA,Widget-A,156,31200,20280
2024-10-04,APAC,Widget-C,89,35600,24040
2024-10-05,AMER,Widget-C,267,106800,72090
[... 49,990 more rows like this]

Can you calculate total revenue by region and find the top 5 products by profit margin?
evals.expected_output: Pre-prompt hook fires and produces an advisory block that: (1) flags HIGH or MEDIUM token cost, (2) detects the data-task pattern (large CSV in context), (3) suggests docker-advisor as a mitigation, (4) does NOT block the prompt (informational only).

assertions.text: Advisory block is present (hook fired) — output contains the '⚡ lqc-tokens advisory' header
Advisory mentions docker-advisor as a suggested mitigation for the data task
Advisory flags this as a data-heavy task (CSV, bulk data, or similar language)
Claude still responds to the actual question (hook does not block the prompt)

evals.name: pre-prompt-hook-vague-prompt
evals.expected_output: Pre-prompt hook detects vague intent ('fix this' with no specific context) and fires an advisory suggesting the user clarify what to fix. The hook should identify this as a low-information prompt that will likely require multiple expensive follow-up turns to resolve.

assertions.text: Advisory block fires for the vague 'fix this' prompt
Advisory identifies vague intent as the anti-pattern (not data-heaviness or token count)
Advisory suggests optimize-prompt skill or asks for clarification

evals.name: pre-prompt-hook-well-formed-prompt-stays-silent
evals.prompt: In src/api/users.ts, the getUser function at line 47 throws a TypeError when userId is null. Add a null guard before the database lookup and return a 404 response. The function signature should stay the same.
evals.expected_output: Pre-prompt hook stays SILENT — this is a well-formed, specific prompt with clear intent and no large data paste. The hook should NOT fire (no advisory output) because the prompt is LOW cost and has no anti-patterns.

assertions.text: No advisory block appears — the hook correctly identifies this as a clean, specific prompt and stays silent
Claude proceeds directly to addressing the null guard fix without any lqc-tokens advisory preamble

evals.name: docker-advisor-falkordb-mcp-path
evals.prompt: I need to analyze a social network dataset. I have 50,000 users and 2 million friendship connections in a CSV. I need to find influencers, communities, and the shortest path between any two users. Please set up the database.
evals.context: mcp__falkordb__list_graphs, mcp__falkordb__query_graph_readonly, mcp__falkordb__query_graph tools are available in the tool list. Docker MCP tools are NOT available.
evals.expected_output: docker-advisor recommends FalkorDB, generates docker-compose.lqc.yml with healthcheck, outputs manual docker compose command (no Docker MCP), then provides MCP-based query examples using mcp__falkordb__query_graph_readonly and mcp__falkordb__query_graph with graphName parameter — NOT Python subprocess code. Also reminds user to update .mcp.json FALKORDB_PORT.

assertions.text: Skill recommends FalkorDB given the graph-shaped social network data
Generated docker-compose.lqc.yml includes a healthcheck block
Query examples reference mcp__falkordb__query_graph_readonly or mcp__falkordb__query_graph with graphName= parameter, NOT Python subprocess
Skill reminds user to update .mcp.json with the assigned FALKORDB_PORT

evals.name: docker-advisor-mongodb-mcp-path
evals.prompt: I have a collection of 30,000 support tickets as JSON. Each ticket has different fields depending on the product. I need to search them by keyword and count by category.
evals.context: mcp__mongodb__find, mcp__mongodb__count, mcp__mongodb__aggregate, mcp__mongodb__collection-schema tools are available. Docker MCP tools are NOT available.
evals.expected_output: docker-advisor recommends MongoDB, generates docker-compose.lqc.yml with healthcheck, provides manual docker compose command, then shows query examples using mcp__mongodb__find and mcp__mongodb__aggregate — NOT pymongo Python code. Reminds user to update .mcp.json connection string port.

assertions.text: Skill recommends MongoDB given the variable-schema JSON document data
Generated docker-compose.lqc.yml includes a healthcheck block
Query examples reference mcp__mongodb__find or mcp__mongodb__aggregate, NOT pymongo subprocess
Skill reminds user to update .mcp.json MongoDB connection string

evals.name: docker-advisor-postgres-mcp-path
evals.prompt: I have Q1 sales data in a CSV: 500,000 rows with date, region, product, units, revenue, cost columns. I need to GROUP BY region and product and find top 10 by margin.
evals.context: mcp__postgres__query tool is available. Docker MCP tools are NOT available.
evals.expected_output: docker-advisor recommends PostgreSQL, generates docker-compose.lqc.yml with healthcheck, provides manual docker compose command, then shows query examples using mcp__postgres__query with SQL — NOT psycopg2 Python code. Notes mcp__postgres__query is read-only (write ops need psycopg2 fallback). Reminds user to update .mcp.json Postgres connection string port.

assertions.text: Skill recommends PostgreSQL given the tabular CSV data with SQL-style access patterns
Generated docker-compose.lqc.yml includes a healthcheck block
Query examples reference mcp__postgres__query with SQL, NOT psycopg2 or subprocess
Skill notes that mcp__postgres__query is read-only and directs user to Python fallback for write operations

evals.name: docker-advisor-isolation-level-prompt
evals.prompt: I have a CSV of 10,000 customer records I need to analyze for this conversation. Please set up the database.
evals.context: No MCP tools available. No base container running (docker ps returns empty). This is a new session with no session_id in lqc-tokens.local.md.
evals.expected_output: docker-advisor asks the isolation level question with session pre-selected as the default. It starts lqc-base-postgres (tabular CSV → Postgres), writes docker-compose.lqc-base.yml with container_name: lqc-base-postgres and port 54012, creates a schema lqc_sess_{id}, and writes session_id + isolated_namespaces entry to lqc-tokens.local.md. Does NOT ask 'Is this data needed after today?' and does NOT create a random-UUID container.

assertions.text: Skill presents isolation level options with session as the recommended/default choice
Generated compose file uses container_name: lqc-base-postgres and port 54012, NOT a random UUID name or random port
Skill creates a Postgres schema with name matching the pattern lqc_sess_* (e.g. lqc_sess_a1b2c3d4) via CREATE SCHEMA or docker exec psql — not a new container or database
Tracking entry written to lqc-tokens.local.md uses isolated_namespaces structure (not ephemeral_containers)
Old 'Is this data needed after today?' question does NOT appear
Output does NOT contain a random UUID container name (pattern: lqc-[a-f0-9]{8}) or random high port (40000-49999)
